category_title: Preschoolers who use unfriendly or bossy language during peer play
scenario_title: Organizing peer play during free play session
minimal_context: Nia is organizing a play activity with her friends during a free play session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Nia, it is time for the free play session. You can go organize a play activity with your friends.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you should do next?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Get out of my way! Your picture is messy and I can do better than you.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Nia, it is time for the free play session. You can go organize a play activity with your friends.

STUDENT: Let me do that.

TUTOR: Keep playing together.

STUDENT: You aren’t playing right. Stop.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: Get out of my way! Your picture is messy and I can do better than you.

task.content: Organizing and participating in play activities with friends.
task.learning_goal: Increasing her positive comments to friends to above 70% of intervals during a 10-min free play monitoring period.

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Sometimes Nia says the first thing she thinks of
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Her tone of voice is often rude and bossy, making impolite or unfriendly statements.

behavior_patterns.condition: When organizing play activities with friends
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Makes a statement in an unfriendly tone of voice, which includes insults, bragging, demands, and/or disrespect
related_quotes: I can do better than you
